heading: Food for progress
label: 1736o Food for progress
notes: References in TextThe Food for Peace Act, referred to in subsecs. (e) and (f)(4), is , , which is classified generally to this chapter (§ 1691 et seq.). Title I of the Act is classified to subchapter II (§ 1701 et seq.) of this chapter. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.act July 10, 1954, ch. 46968 Stat. 454section 1691 of this title
CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Food Security Act of 1985, and not as part of the Food for Peace Act which comprises this chapter.

L. 107–171, § 3106(b)(1)“(b) In order to use the food resources of the United States more effectively in support of developing countries, and countries that are emerging democracies, that have made commitments to introduce or expand free enterprise elements in their agricultural economies through changes in commodity pricing, marketing, input availability, distribution, and private sector involvement, the President is authorized to enter into agreements with the governments of such countries (including the independent states of the former Soviet Union), or with private voluntary organizations, nonprofit agricultural organizations, cooperatives, intergovernmental organizations, or other private entities, to furnish commodities made available pursuant to subsections (e) and (f) of this section. Such agreements may provide for commodities to be furnished on a multiyear basis.“(c) As used in this section, the term ‘commodities’ means agricultural commodities and the products thereof.”Subsec. children.heading: Short title
children.label: a Short title
children.text: This section may be cited as the “Food for Progress Act of 1985”.

children.heading: Cooperative
children.label: 1 Cooperative
children.text: The term “cooperative” has the meaning given the term in section 402 of the Food for Peace Act ().7 U.S.C. 1732

children.heading: Corporation
children.label: 2 Corporation
children.text: The term “Corporation” means the Commodity Credit Corporation.

children.heading: Developing country
children.label: 3 Developing country
children.text: The term “developing country” has the meaning given the term in section 402 of the Food for Peace Act ().7 U.S.C. 1732

children.heading: Eligible commodity
children.label: 4 Eligible commodity
children.text: The term “eligible commodity” means an agricultural commodity, or a product of an agricultural commodity, in inventories of the Corporation or acquired by the Secretary or the Corporation for disposition through commercial purchases under a program authorized under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: the government of an emerging agricultural country;

children.label: B
children.text: an intergovernmental organization;

children.label: C
children.text: a private voluntary organization;

children.label: D
children.text: a nonprofit agricultural organization or cooperative;

children.label: E
children.text: a nongovernmental organization;

children.label: F
children.text: a college or university (as such terms are defined in ); andsection 3103(4) of this title

children.label: G
children.text: any other private entity.

children.heading: Food security
children.label: 6 Food security
children.text: The term “food security” means access by all people at all times to sufficient food and nutrition for a healthy and productive life.

children.heading: Nongovernmental organization
children.label: 7 Nongovernmental organization
children.text: The term “nongovernmental organization” has the meaning given the term in section 402 of the Food for Peace Act ().7 U.S.C. 1732

children.heading: Private voluntary organization
children.label: 8 Private voluntary organization
children.text: The term “private voluntary organization” has the meaning given the term in section 402 of the Food for Peace Act ().7 U.S.C. 1732

children.heading: Program
children.label: 9 Program
children.text: The term “program” means a food assistance or development initiative proposed by an eligible entity and approved by the Secretary under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: the proceeds eligible entities generate through monetization of such commodity, bears to

children.label: B
children.text: the cost to the Federal Government to procure and ship the commodity to the country where it is monetized.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 11 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Agriculture.

children.heading: Program
children.label: c Program
children.text: In order to use the food resources of the United States more effectively in support of developing countries, and countries that are emerging democracies that have made commitments to introduce or expand free enterprise elements in their agricultural economies through changes in commodity pricing, marketing, input availability, distribution, and private sector involvement, the Secretary shall enter into agreements with eligible entities to furnish to the countries eligible commodities made available under subsections (e) and (f).
access, on the part of farmers in the country, to private, competitive markets for their product;
market pricing of eligible commodities to foster adequate private sector incentives to individual farmers to produce food on a regular basis for the country’s domestic needs;
establishment of market-determined foreign exchange rates;
timely availability of production inputs (such as seed, fertilizer, or pesticides) to farmers;
access to technologies appropriate to the level of agricultural development in the country; and
construction of facilities and distribution systems necessary to handle perishable products.
The Corporation shall make available to the Secretary such eligible commodities as the Secretary may request for purposes of furnishing eligible commodities under this section.

Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Corporation may use funds appropriated to carry out title I of the Food for Peace Act [ et seq.] in carrying out this section with respect to eligible commodities made available under that Act [], and subsection (g) does not apply to eligible commodities furnished on a grant basis or on credit terms under that title.7 U.S.C. 17017 U.S.C. 1691

The Corporation may finance the sale and exportation of eligible commodities, made available under the Food for Peace Act [ et seq.], which are furnished under this section. Payment for eligible commodities made available under that Act which are purchased on credit terms under this section shall be on the same basis as the terms provided in section 103 of that Act [].7 U.S.C. 16917 U.S.C. 1703

In the case of eligible commodities made available under the Food for Peace Act for purposes of this section, section 406 of that Act [] shall apply to eligible commodities furnished on a grant basis under this section and sections 402, 403(a), 403(c), and 403(i) of that Act [, 1733(a), (c), (i)] shall apply to all eligible commodities furnished under this section.7 U.S.C. 17367 U.S.C. 1732

children.heading: .—No effect on domestic programs
children.label: 5 .—No effect on domestic programs
children.text: The Secretary shall not make an eligible commodity available for disposition under this section in any amount that will reduce the amount of the eligible commodity that is traditionally made available through donations to domestic feeding programs or agencies, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Provision of eligible commodities to developing countries
children.label: f Provision of eligible commodities to developing countries
A
children.text: grants, or

children.label: B
children.text: sales on credit terms,

children.label: A
children.text: the Corporation does not hold stocks of such eligible commodities; or

children.label: B
children.text: Corporation stocks are insufficient to satisfy commitments made in agreements entered into under this section and such eligible commodities are needed to fulfill such commitments.

No funds of the Corporation in excess of $40,000,000 (exclusive of the cost of eligible commodities) may be used for each of fiscal years 1996 through 2023 to carry out this section with respect to eligible commodities made available under unless authorized in advance in appropriation Acts.section 1431(b) of this title

The cost of eligible commodities made available under which are furnished under this section, and the expenses incurred in connection with furnishing such eligible commodities, shall be in addition to the level of assistance programmed under the Food for Peace Act [ et seq.] and may not be considered expenditures for international affairs and finance.section 1431(b) of this title7 U.S.C. 1691

children.heading: .—Sale procedure
children.label: 5 .—Sale procedure
children.text: In making sales of eligible eligible commodities under this section, the Secretary shall follow the sale procedure described in section 403() of the Food for Peace Act [()].17 U.S.C. 1733So in original.1ll

children.heading: Minimum tonnage
children.label: g Minimum tonnage
children.text: Subject to subsection (f)(3), not less than 400,000 metric tons of eligible commodities shall be provided under this section for the program for each of fiscal years 2002 through 2023.

children.heading: Prohibition on resale or transshipment of eligible commodities
children.label: h Prohibition on resale or transshipment of eligible commodities
children.text: An agreement entered into under this section shall prohibit the resale or transshipment of the eligible commodities provided under the agreement to other countries.

children.heading: Displacement of United States commercial sales
children.label: i Displacement of United States commercial sales
children.text: In entering into agreements under this section, the Secretary shall take reasonable steps to avoid displacement of any sales of United States commodities that would otherwise be made to such countries.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In carrying out this section, the Secretary, on request and subject to the availability of eligible commodities, is encouraged to approve agreements that provide for eligible commodities to be made available for distribution or sale by the recipient on a multicountry or multiyear basis if the agreements otherwise meet the requirements of this section.

children.label: A
children.text: make all determinations concerning program agreements and resource requests for programs under this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: announce those determinations.

children.label: A
children.text: a list of programs, countries, and eligible commodities, and the total amount of funds for transportation and administrative costs, approved during the prior fiscal year under this section;

children.label: i
children.text: factors that influenced the rate of return; and

children.label: ii
children.text: with respect to the commodity, the costs of bagging or further processing, ocean transportation, inland transportation, storage costs, and any other information that the Secretary determines to be necessary; and

children.label: C
children.text: for each instance in which a commodity was made available under this section at a rate of return less than 70 percent, an explanation for the rate of return realized.

children.heading: Effective and termination dates
children.label: k Effective and termination dates
children.text: This section shall be effective during the period beginning , and ending .October 1, 1985December 31, 2023

To enhance the development of private sector agriculture in countries receiving assistance under this section the Secretary may, in each of the fiscal years 1996 through 2023, use in addition to any amounts or eligible commodities otherwise made available under this section for such activities, not to exceed $15,000,000 (or, in the case of fiscal year 1999, $12,000,000) of Corporation funds (or eligible commodities of an equal value owned by the Corporation), to provide assistance in the administration, sale, and monitoring of food assistance programs, and to provide technical assistance for monetization programs, to strengthen private sector agriculture in recipient countries.

To carry out this subsection, the Secretary may provide eligible commodities under agreements entered into under this section in a manner that uses the commodity transaction as a means of developing in the recipient countries a competitive private sector that can provide for the importation, transportation, storage, marketing and distribution of such eligible commodities.

The Secretary may use the assistance provided under this subsection and proceeds derived from the sale of eligible commodities under paragraph (2) to design, monitor, and administer activities undertaken with such assistance, for the purpose of strengthening or creating the capacity of recipient country private enterprises to undertake commercial transactions, with the overall goal of increasing potential markets for United States agricultural eligible commodities.

children.label: i
children.text: programs targeted at hunger and malnutrition; or

children.label: ii
children.text: development programs involving food security;

children.label: B
children.text: transportation, storage, and distribution of eligible commodities provided under this section; and

children.label: C
children.text: administration, sales, monitoring, and technical assistance.
the sale of eligible commodities, including the marketing of eligible commodities through the private sector; and

the use of the proceeds generated in the humanitarian and development programs of such agricultural trade organizations, intergovernmental organizations, private voluntary organizations, and cooperatives.

children.label: i
children.text: in an effective manner;

children.label: ii
children.text: in the areas of greatest need; and

children.label: iii
children.text: in a manner that promotes the purposes of this section;

children.label: B
children.text: in using eligible commodities, assesses and takes into account the needs of recipient countries and the target populations of the recipient countries;

children.label: C
children.text: works with recipient countries, and indigenous institutions or groups in recipient countries, to design and carry out mutually acceptable programs authorized under this section; and

children.label: D
children.text: monitors and reports on the distribution or sale of eligible commodities provided under this section using methods that, as determined by the Secretary, facilitate accurate and timely reporting.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 270 days after , the Secretary shall review and, as necessary, make changes in regulations and internal procedures designed to streamline, improve, and clarify the application, approval, and implementation processes pertaining to agreements under this section.May 13, 2002

children.label: i
children.text: revising procedures for submitting proposals;

children.label: ii
children.text: developing criteria for program approval that separately address the objectives of the program;

children.label: iii
children.text: pre-screening organizations and proposals to ensure that the minimum qualifications are met;

children.label: iv
children.text: implementing e-government initiatives and otherwise improving the efficiency of the proposal submission and approval processes;

children.label: v
children.text: upgrading information management systems;

children.label: vi
children.text: improving commodity and transportation procurement processes; and

children.label: vii
children.text: ensuring that evaluation and monitoring methods are sufficient.

children.heading: Consultations
children.label: C Consultations
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Secretary shall consult with the Committee on Agriculture, and the Committee on International Relations, of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry of the Senate on changes made in regulations and procedures.May 13, 2002

children.heading: Reports
children.label: 3 Reports
children.text: Each eligible entity that enters into an agreement under this section shall submit to the Secretary, at such time as the Secretary may request, a report containing such information as the Secretary may request relating to the use of eligible commodities and funds furnished to the eligible entity under this section.

children.heading: Private voluntary organizations and other private entities
children.label: o Private voluntary organizations and other private entities

children.text: shall enter into agreements with eligible entities described in subparagraphs (C) and (G) of subsection (b)(5); and
shall not discriminate against such eligible entities.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For each of fiscal years 2019 through 2023, subject to the availability of appropriations pursuant to the authorization in paragraph (3), the Secretary shall enter into 1 or more pilot agreements with 1 or more eligible entities through which the Secretary shall provide financial assistance to the eligible entities to carry out activities consistent with subsection ()(4)(A).l

children.label: A
children.text: the amount provided to eligible entities under each pilot agreement pursuant to paragraph (1) and how the funds were used;

children.label: B
children.text: the activities carried out under each pilot agreement;

children.label: C
children.text: the number of direct and indirect beneficiaries of those activities; and

children.label: D
children.text: the effectiveness of the pilot agreements, including as applicable the impact on food security and agricultural productivity.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 3 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out pilot agreements pursuant to this subsection $10,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2019 through 2023.
